svg.text: Understanding Electron Configurations & the Table's Shape
Electrons orbit the nucleus in shells, divided into subshells (s, p, d, f). Each subshell holds a fixed max number of electrons.
The periodic table is literally a map of which subshell is being filled — its geometry is driven by quantum mechanics:
Noble gas shorthand: configs are abbreviated starting from the nearest preceding noble gas.
E.g. Gold (Au, Z=79): full config is 1s² 2s² 2p⁶ … 5d¹⁰ — written as [Xe] 4f¹⁴ 5d¹⁰ 6s¹
where [Xe] stands for all 54 electrons of Xenon's complete configuration.
Why are lanthanides & actinides in a sub-table? In periods 6 and 7, the 4f / 5f subshell fills
between the s-block (cols 1–2) and the d-block (col 4 onward). Inserting them inline would
make the table 32 columns wide. By convention they are extracted into a separate f-block panel below.
